ebrdify 0.5.1
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology

canonise.Rd

Rewrites country identifiers (names, ISO3 or ISO2 codes) to the official country names used in the EBRD Transition Report. This enforces the TR house style and the "forbidden names" rules from Annex I of the style guide — for example:

Usage

canonise(x, from = NULL, warn = TRUE)

Arguments

x

A vector of country identifiers (names, ISO3, or ISO2 codes).

from

Format of x: "country.name", "iso3c", or "iso2c". If NULL (default) the format is auto-detected.

warn

If TRUE (default), report identifiers that could not be matched via message().

Value

A character vector the same length as x of official EBRD/TR names, with NA for identifiers that could not be matched. Designed to drop into dplyr::mutate(), e.g. mutate(country = canonise(country)).

Details

  • Czech Republic \(\rightarrow\) Czechia

  • Palestine \(\rightarrow\) West Bank and Gaza

  • Kyrgyzstan \(\rightarrow\) Kyrgyz Republic

  • Slovakia \(\rightarrow\) Slovak Republic

  • Turkey \(\rightarrow\) Türkiye

  • Ivory Coast \(\rightarrow\) Côte d'Ivoire

  • Taiwan \(\rightarrow\) Taipei China

EBRD economies are named from the official Annex I list; other economies fall back to countrycode's English short name, with TR-specific overrides applied on top (Taipei China, Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR, Czechia) because countrycode returns a non-TR form for those.

See also

ebrdify() to classify economies and list_ebrd() for the full list of EBRD economies.

Examples

canonise(c("Czech Republic", "Palestine", "Kyrgyzstan", "Taiwan"))
#> [1] "Czechia"            "West Bank and Gaza" "Kyrgyz Republic"   
#> [4] "Taipei China"      
canonise(c("TUR", "SVK", "CIV"), from = "iso3c")
#> [1] "Türkiye"         "Slovak Republic" "Côte d'Ivoire"  
canonise(c("Germany", "Wakanda"))   # non-EBRD pass through; unmatched -> NA
#> The following entries could not be matched: Wakanda
#> [1] "Germany" NA
